mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0% after the signal activates.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production growth outpaces consumer credit expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional correlation between the INDPRO-RSAFS spread and 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in industrial production capacity utilization signals improved corporate operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO rebounds are statistically indistinguishable from baseline returns during non-recovery periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces the equity risk premium as macroeconomic uncertainty declines, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of quarterly GDPC1 growth against 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, tight monetary conditions increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and rising housing starts signal robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days by expanding corporate cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In high-inflation regimes where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, real demand contraction forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy aggressively, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.0%, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during actual high-inflation episodes (t-test p > 0.05).
